Regarding contracts and amount-based support materials, no signed contract was included, the contract did not carry the university seal, and no construction contract was attached.
No large-sum payment application was included, and no related leader signature or stamp was shown on that form.

case_ch:
In May 2026, the Experimental Governance Studio processed a reimbursement case for project wrap-up work. The materials were compiled by Dr. Zhang, who handled the paperwork under the university's regular reimbursement process.
For the basic filing steps, the appointment reimbursement form had been printed, the fund ledger entry was submitted, the form carried the project leader's signature, the handler signed the form, and the project leader information matched the system record.
For signatures and supporting approvals, no separate authorization letter from the project leader was on file, the original receipts were signed by the project leader, the original receipts were also signed by the handler, single-pen approval for university funds was included, a research-project leader signature was not provided as the approval basis, and no department approval for special transactions was attached.
The filing selected electronic invoices in the form, and the corresponding electronic invoices were verified in the system.
The packet also included travel approval materials.
The payment amount was at least RMB 30,000 but did not exceed RMB 50,000.
Regarding contracts and amount-based support materials, a signed contract was included, the contract carried the university seal, and no construction contract was attached.
No large-sum payment application was included, and no related leader signature or stamp was shown on that form.

In October 2026, the University Archives Digitization Team prepared small procurement and reimbursement materials for day-to-day project operations. The reimbursement package was organized by Dr. Zhao around one round of internal purchasing and related support expenses.
The spending categories mentioned in the file included office stationery.
For asset-classification and procurement support materials, the purchase was treated as a low-value consumable, Asset Management Office review was not required, no external purchase inventory review form was attached, a seller goods list was provided, no online shopping transaction record was provided, and no fixed-asset addition slip was attached.
For book-management details, no detailed list for book-related expense was provided, no single book item was marked at RMB 1,000 or above, and the books were not shown as registered with the library.
For printing and publication support details, no single printing invoice exceeded RMB 1,000, there were no multiple near-time printing invoices together exceeding RMB 1,000, no detailed list for printing was attached, and the invoice amounts did not appear to be artificially split.
For monograph-publication contract control, no monograph-publication contract was included, the contract counterparty was not marked as an individual or department, and no approval from the project-fund administrator was shown on the contract.

case_ch:
In August 2026, the Intelligent Sensing Lab submitted a batch of service invoices and local transport receipts for routine reimbursement review. The materials were assembled by Dr. Chen, who sent the packet to finance for a line-by-line review of the original receipts.
During the invoice review, the invoice showed the required supervision seal, the business scope on the invoice matched the reimbursement purpose, the invoice content was complete, the invoice date was present, the quantity field was filled in, the unit-price field was missing, the amount field was filled in, the item description was present, the payee seal was present, the numeric and written amounts were consistent, the invoice showed no signs of alteration, the invoice did not appear to be forged, the invoice showed no obvious indicators of fraudulent reimbursement risk, the taxi receipts did not show suspiciously consecutive serial numbers, there was no unusual concentration of taxi receipts from the same vehicle, and the reimbursement procedure was complete.
The review therefore highlighted the following problematic points: the unit-price field was missing.
The spending categories mentioned in the file included office stationery and cleaning supplies.
For asset-classification and procurement support materials, the purchase was treated as a low-value consumable, Asset Management Office review was not required, no external purchase inventory review form was attached, no seller goods list was provided, an online shopping transaction record was provided, and no fixed-asset addition slip was attached.
For book-management details, no detailed list for book-related expense was provided, no single book item was marked at RMB 1,000 or above, and the books were not shown as registered with the library.
For printing and publication support details, no single printing invoice exceeded RMB 1,000, there were no multiple near-time printing invoices together exceeding RMB 1,000, no detailed list for printing was attached, and the invoice amounts did not appear to be artificially split.
For monograph-publication contract control, no monograph-publication contract was included, the contract counterparty was not marked as an individual or department, and no approval from the project-fund administrator was shown on the contract.

case_ch:
In February 2026, the Intelligent Sensing Lab arranged a business trip to Qingdao for on-site testing preparation. The reimbursement was prepared by Dr. Chen, and the case was filed under the travel-expense rules for bureau-level or equivalent personnel using research project funds.
On the trip-approval side, a trip report form was included; the form was signed by the unit head and stamped with the department or first-level budget-unit seal; this was not the unit head's own trip; no separate other-leader signature for a unit-head self-trip was shown.
Itinerary and transport details: The case included intercity transportation expenses; lodging expenses were also claimed; city transportation expenses were claimed as well; The chosen long-distance mode of travel was train; the reported ticket class was a soft sleeper, a first-class soft seat, or first class on a high-speed train; the file contained no separate contract-specific transport rule.
Transport-standard handling: the selected transport class was not marked as above the usual standard; no special explanation for the transport arrangement was attached; no project leader signature for a transport exception was shown; the travel arrangement was not stated to satisfy any contract-specific transport rule.
Lodging details: the traveler was reviewed against the bureau-level lodging standard; the nightly lodging amount was RMB 580; the stay lasted 2 night(s); the trip took place in month 8. A lodging invoice was provided.
Supporting documents for transport and lodging: an intercity transport invoice was provided; the case was not described as remote research with unobtainable transport tickets; no approved special explanation for missing transport tickets was attached; no other valid supporting documents for missing transport tickets were attached; the lodging invoice was not described as unobtainable; no approved special explanation for missing lodging documentation was attached; no handwritten receipt signed by the payee was attached.
Meal and local transport reimbursement: the trip covered 3 calendar day(s) in other regions; the claimed meal subsidy total was RMB 300; the actual claimed city-transport total was RMB 180; the city-transport lump-sum claim was RMB 0. The file did not use the city-transport lump-sum method. City-transport receipts were provided. The city-transport claim was kept within budget.
Cost-sharing with external units: both intercity transport and lodging were not jointly marked as paid by an external unit; neither side of the file said that only one of intercity transport or lodging was paid by an external unit; no signed special explanation for partial external support was attached; no valid notice or invitation supporting partial external funding was attached; the file did not say that transport or meal costs had already been paid by an external unit; the traveler was not marked as claiming transport or meal costs again through the university.
Self-drive and rental details: no self-drive or rental car use was involved; no signed special explanation for self-drive or rental use was attached; the stated fuel-and-toll total for a rental or self-drive arrangement was RMB 0; the public-transport lowest-standard comparison amount was RMB 0.
External-invitee status: the case did not involve an external invitee; there was no project-leader confirmation for an external invitee's transport and lodging arrangement; no meal subsidy was claimed for an external invitee; no city-transport lump sum was claimed for an external invitee.

case_ch:
In May 2026, the Smart Finance Research Group arranged a business trip to Beijing for conference-related academic exchange. The reimbursement was prepared by Dr. Xu, and the case was filed under the travel-expense rules for bureau-level or equivalent personnel using research project funds.
On the trip-approval side, a trip report form was included; the form was signed by the unit head and stamped with the department or first-level budget-unit seal; this was not the unit head's own trip; no separate other-leader signature for a unit-head self-trip was shown.
Itinerary and transport details: The case included intercity transportation expenses; lodging expenses were also claimed; city transportation expenses were claimed as well; The chosen long-distance mode of travel was air travel; the reported ticket class was business class; the file contained no separate contract-specific transport rule.
Transport-standard handling: the selected transport class was not marked as above the usual standard; no special explanation for the transport arrangement was attached; no project leader signature for a transport exception was shown; the travel arrangement was not stated to satisfy any contract-specific transport rule.
Lodging details: the traveler was reviewed against the lodging standard for other personnel; the nightly lodging amount was RMB 450; the stay lasted 3 night(s); the trip took place in month 5. A lodging invoice was provided.
Supporting documents for transport and lodging: an intercity transport invoice was provided; the case was not described as remote research with unobtainable transport tickets; no approved special explanation for missing transport tickets was attached; no other valid supporting documents for missing transport tickets were attached; the lodging invoice was not described as unobtainable; no approved special explanation for missing lodging documentation was attached; no handwritten receipt signed by the payee was attached.
Meal and local transport reimbursement: the trip covered 3 calendar day(s) in other regions; the claimed meal subsidy total was RMB 240; the actual claimed city-transport total was RMB 150; the city-transport lump-sum claim was RMB 0. The file did not use the city-transport lump-sum method. City-transport receipts were provided. The city-transport claim was kept within budget.
Cost-sharing with external units: both intercity transport and lodging were not jointly marked as paid by an external unit; neither side of the file said that only one of intercity transport or lodging was paid by an external unit; no signed special explanation for partial external support was attached; no valid notice or invitation supporting partial external funding was attached; the file did not say that transport or meal costs had already been paid by an external unit; the traveler was not marked as claiming transport or meal costs again through the university.
Self-drive and rental details: no self-drive or rental car use was involved; no signed special explanation for self-drive or rental use was attached; the stated fuel-and-toll total for a rental or self-drive arrangement was RMB 0; the public-transport lowest-standard comparison amount was RMB 0.
External-invitee status: the case did not involve an external invitee; there was no project-leader confirmation for an external invitee's transport and lodging arrangement; no meal subsidy was claimed for an external invitee; no city-transport lump sum was claimed for an external invitee.

On the trip-approval side, a trip report form was included; the form was signed by the unit head and stamped with the department or first-level budget-unit seal; this was not the unit head's own trip; no separate other-leader signature for a unit-head self-trip was shown.
Itinerary and transport details: The case included intercity transportation expenses; lodging expenses were also claimed; city transportation expenses were claimed as well; The chosen long-distance mode of travel was air travel; the reported ticket class was economy class; the file contained no separate contract-specific transport rule.
Transport-standard handling: the selected transport class was not marked as above the usual standard; no special explanation for the transport arrangement was attached; no project leader signature for a transport exception was shown; the travel arrangement was not stated to satisfy any contract-specific transport rule.
Lodging details: the traveler was reviewed against the lodging standard for other personnel; the nightly lodging amount was RMB 480; the stay lasted 3 night(s); the trip took place in month 4. A lodging invoice was provided.
Supporting documents for transport and lodging: no intercity transport invoice was provided; the case was described as remote research where transport tickets could not be obtained; an approved special explanation for missing transport tickets was attached; other valid supporting documents for missing transport tickets were attached; the lodging invoice was not described as unobtainable; no approved special explanation for missing lodging documentation was attached; no handwritten receipt signed by the payee was attached.
Meal and local transport reimbursement: the trip covered 3 calendar day(s) in other regions; the claimed meal subsidy total was RMB 240; the actual claimed city-transport total was RMB 150; the city-transport lump-sum claim was RMB 0. The file did not use the city-transport lump-sum method. City-transport receipts were provided. The city-transport claim was kept within budget.
Cost-sharing with external units: both intercity transport and lodging were not jointly marked as paid by an external unit; neither side of the file said that only one of intercity transport or lodging was paid by an external unit; no signed special explanation for partial external support was attached; no valid notice or invitation supporting partial external funding was attached; the file did not say that transport or meal costs had already been paid by an external unit; the traveler was not marked as claiming transport or meal costs again through the university.
Self-drive and rental details: no self-drive or rental car use was involved; no signed special explanation for self-drive or rental use was attached; the stated fuel-and-toll total for a rental or self-drive arrangement was RMB 0; the public-transport lowest-standard comparison amount was RMB 0.
External-invitee status: the case did not involve an external invitee; there was no project-leader confirmation for an external invitee's transport and lodging arrangement; no meal subsidy was claimed for an external invitee; no city-transport lump sum was claimed for an external invitee.

case_ch: In August 2026, the Digital Humanities Project Group arranged an overseas trip for academic exchange, collaboration meetings, and related professional activities, and the traveler submitted the reimbursement after returning. A Foreign Affairs approval document was available. An original overseas expense statement was provided. The expense statement included conference registration fees, included living allowances, included incidental expenses, and included inter-city transportation expenses. Supporting receipts included airfare records with boarding passes. They did not include accommodation receipts, conference registration receipts, inter-city transport receipts, visa receipts, and insurance receipts. The overseas trip receipts were reviewed by the Foreign Affairs travel service center and were signed off there. The trip was charged to non-research project funds. The traveler was classified as division-chief-level and below personnel. The itinerary involved economy-class air travel, hard-seat rail travel, and third-class ship travel. International airfare was paid by business card, and accommodation was paid by bank transfer. The reimbursement was completed after the traveler returned. The file did not include expenses that had been prepaid for future travel. No airfare advance, accommodation advance, special advance circumstance, or stay longer than one month was indicated. The case states that all overseas expenses were reimbursed together in one filing. The case did not mention any separate overseas purchases of books or materials. The reimbursement appointment selected the relevant foreign currency. The system converted the amount into RMB using the applicable exchange rate.

case_ch: In August 2026, the Intelligent Sensing Lab arranged an overseas trip for academic exchange, collaboration meetings, and related professional activities, and the traveler submitted the reimbursement after returning. A Foreign Affairs approval document was available. An original overseas expense statement was provided. The expense statement included conference registration fees, included living allowances, included incidental expenses, and included inter-city transportation expenses. Supporting receipts included airfare records with boarding passes. They did not include accommodation receipts, conference registration receipts, inter-city transport receipts, visa receipts, and insurance receipts. The overseas trip receipts were reviewed by the Foreign Affairs travel service center and were signed off there. The trip was charged to non-research project funds. The traveler was classified as bureau-director-level personnel. The itinerary involved business-class air travel, and second-class high-speed rail travel. International airfare was paid by business card, and accommodation was paid by bank transfer. The reimbursement was completed after the traveler returned. The file did not include expenses that had been prepaid for future travel. No airfare advance, accommodation advance, special advance circumstance, or stay longer than one month was indicated. The case states that all overseas expenses were reimbursed together in one filing. The case did not mention any separate overseas purchases of books or materials. The reimbursement appointment selected the relevant foreign currency. The system converted the amount into RMB using the applicable exchange rate.

It labeled the event as a domestic business conference, set the domestic class field to Class 1, set the international class field to International Class 2, recorded 100 attendees, and listed 2 conference day(s) with 2 day(s) of duration in total. Lodging was arranged and meals were arranged. The claimed actual cost per person per day was RMB 480 for lodging, RMB 150 for meals, and RMB 100 for other conference expenses, for a stated total of RMB 730. Excluded items were kept within budget. The reimbursement packet included a notice and agenda, a budget form, an expense detail list, a sign-in sheet showing attendee organizations, service invoices, original supporting documents, e-settlement records, and the related contract or agreement. The conference expenses were reimbursed in a single consolidated batch. It was not described as a regular work meeting or seminar, and no separate work-meal claim was filed. An advance payment was requested at 20% of the budget. For the advance, the file included a notice and agenda, included a budget form, included the related contract, and did not include international-conference approval where applicable. For foreign-attendee issues, the file says foreign guests were not present at this domestic conference, and Foreign Affairs Office approval was not shown. The attendee mix showed 40% from Shanghai-based institutions. The meeting was not held outside Shanghai, and the venue was not a prohibited scenic area. Operational details in the file listed venue rent at RMB 200 per person per day, equipment rent at RMB 100 per person per day, cold meals at RMB 180 per person per serving, and other service costs at RMB 100 per person per day. The staffing arrangement involved 8 staff members, including 3 resident staff, with staff board set at RMB 400 per person per day and volunteer subsidies at RMB 80 per person per day. If interpretation was needed, oral interpretation was set at RMB 5000 per person per day, written translation at RMB 180 per thousand Chinese characters, and simultaneous-interpretation equipment plus office rental at RMB 100 per person per day. The UN-language interpretation flag was set to true. Vehicle arrangements were capped at RMB 1000 per vehicle per day, with 20 approved seats.

The claimed actual cost per person per day was RMB 340 for lodging, RMB 130 for meals, and RMB 80 for other conference expenses, for a stated total of RMB 550. Excluded items were kept within budget. The reimbursement packet included a notice and agenda, a budget form, an expense detail list, a sign-in sheet showing attendee organizations, service invoices, original supporting documents, e-settlement records, and the related contract or agreement. The conference expenses were reimbursed in a single consolidated batch. It was not described as a regular work meeting or seminar, and no separate work-meal claim was filed. The staffing arrangement involved 8 staff members, including 3 resident staff, with staff board set at RMB 400 per person per day and volunteer subsidies at RMB 80 per person per day. If interpretation was needed, oral interpretation was set at RMB 5000 per person per day, written translation at RMB 180 per thousand Chinese characters, and simultaneous-interpretation equipment plus office rental at RMB 100 per person per day. The UN-language interpretation flag was set to true. Vehicle arrangements were capped at RMB 1000 per vehicle per day, with 20 approved seats.

case_ch: In August 2026, the Life and Health Interdisciplinary Team organized a domestic business conference to review project progress, service coordination, and implementation details. The form states that research funds would be used. It labeled the event as a domestic business conference, set the domestic class field to Class 1, set the international class field to International Class 2, recorded 100 attendees, and listed 2 conference day(s) with 2 day(s) of duration in total. Lodging was arranged and meals were arranged. The claimed actual cost per person per day was RMB 500 for lodging, RMB 150 for meals, and RMB 110 for other conference expenses, for a stated total of RMB 760. Excluded items were kept within budget. The reimbursement packet included a notice and agenda, a budget form, an expense detail list, a sign-in sheet showing attendee organizations, service invoices, original supporting documents, e-settlement records, and the related contract or agreement. The conference expenses were reimbursed in a single consolidated batch. It was not described as a regular work meeting or seminar, and no separate work-meal claim was filed. No advance payment was requested. For foreign-attendee issues, the file says foreign guests were not present at this domestic conference, and Foreign Affairs Office approval was not shown. The attendee mix showed 40% from Shanghai-based institutions. The meeting was not held outside Shanghai, and the venue was not a prohibited scenic area. Operational details in the file listed venue rent at RMB 200 per person per day, equipment rent at RMB 100 per person per day, cold meals at RMB 180 per person per serving, and other service costs at RMB 100 per person per day. The staffing arrangement involved 8 staff members, including 3 resident staff, with staff board set at RMB 400 per person per day and volunteer subsidies at RMB 80 per person per day. If interpretation was needed, oral interpretation was set at RMB 5000 per person per day, written translation at RMB 180 per thousand Chinese characters, and simultaneous-interpretation equipment plus office rental at RMB 100 per person per day. The UN-language interpretation flag was set to true. Vehicle arrangements were capped at RMB 1000 per vehicle per day, with 20 approved seats.

case_ch: In March 2026, the Cognitive Science Team organized a domestic business conference to review project progress, service coordination, and implementation details. The form states that research funds would be used. It labeled the event as a domestic business conference, set the domestic class field to Class 1, set the international class field to International Class 2, recorded 100 attendees, and listed 2 conference day(s) with 2 day(s) of duration in total. Lodging was arranged and meals were arranged. The claimed actual cost per person per day was RMB 480 for lodging, RMB 150 for meals, and RMB 100 for other conference expenses, for a stated total of RMB 730. Excluded items were not kept within budget. The reimbursement packet included a notice and agenda, a budget form, an expense detail list, a sign-in sheet showing attendee organizations, service invoices, original supporting documents, e-settlement records, and the related contract or agreement. The conference expenses were reimbursed in a single consolidated batch. It was not described as a regular work meeting or seminar, and no separate work-meal claim was filed. No advance payment was requested. For foreign-attendee issues, the file says foreign guests were not present at this domestic conference, and Foreign Affairs Office approval was not shown. The attendee mix showed 40% from Shanghai-based institutions. The meeting was not held outside Shanghai, and the venue was not a prohibited scenic area. Operational details in the file listed venue rent at RMB 200 per person per day, equipment rent at RMB 100 per person per day, cold meals at RMB 180 per person per serving, and other service costs at RMB 100 per person per day. The staffing arrangement involved 8 staff members, including 3 resident staff, with staff board set at RMB 400 per person per day and volunteer subsidies at RMB 80 per person per day. If interpretation was needed, oral interpretation was set at RMB 5000 per person per day, written translation at RMB 180 per thousand Chinese characters, and simultaneous-interpretation equipment plus office rental at RMB 100 per person per day. The UN-language interpretation flag was set to true. Vehicle arrangements were capped at RMB 1000 per vehicle per day, with 20 approved seats.

case_ch: In August 2026, the Urban Computing Group organized an international conference in China for academic exchange, collaboration planning, and meeting services. The form states that non-research funds would be used. It labeled the event as an international conference held in China, set the domestic class field to Class 1, set the international class field to International Class 2, recorded 100 attendees, and listed 2 conference day(s) with 2 day(s) of duration in total. Lodging was arranged and meals were arranged. The claimed actual cost per person per day was RMB 300 for lodging, RMB 200 for meals, and RMB 250 for other conference expenses, for a stated total of RMB 750. Excluded items were kept within budget. The reimbursement packet included a notice and agenda, a budget form, an expense detail list, a sign-in sheet showing attendee organizations, service invoices, original supporting documents, e-settlement records, and the related contract or agreement. The conference expenses were reimbursed in a single consolidated batch. It was not described as a regular work meeting or seminar, and no separate work-meal claim was filed. An advance payment was requested at 25% of the budget. For the advance, the file included a notice and agenda, included a budget form, included the related contract, and did not include international-conference approval where applicable. The case was treated as an international conference held in China, and separate Foreign Affairs Office approval was not shown. The attendee mix showed 40% from Shanghai-based institutions. The meeting was not held outside Shanghai, and the venue was not a prohibited scenic area. Operational details in the file listed venue rent at RMB 200 per person per day, equipment rent at RMB 100 per person per day, cold meals at RMB 220 per person per serving, and other service costs at RMB 100 per person per day. The staffing arrangement involved 10 staff members, including 5 resident staff, with staff board set at RMB 450 per person per day and volunteer subsidies at RMB 100 per person per day. If interpretation was needed, oral interpretation was set at RMB 6000 per person per day, written translation at RMB 200 per thousand Chinese characters, and simultaneous-interpretation equipment plus office rental at RMB 100 per person per day. The UN-language interpretation flag was set to false. Vehicle arrangements were capped at RMB 1500 per vehicle per day, with 30 approved seats.

The training lasted 20 day(s). The file did not mark the program as exceeding 30 days, and the number of days beyond the 30-day threshold was recorded as 0.
For the main training expenses, accommodation was listed at RMB 10000, meals at RMB 3000, venue/materials/transport at RMB 1600, and other expenses at RMB 600, for a reported training total of RMB 15200. Instructor fees were included, and the training-level instructor-fee entry was RMB 2000. 
The quota references in the file were RMB 500 for accommodation, RMB 150 for meals, RMB 80 for venue/materials/transport, RMB 30 for other expenses, and RMB 760 as the total quota standard.
Supporting materials for the training reimbursement: a training-expense detailed list was attached; a trainee list with contact information was attached; training service-provider receipts were attached.
Instructor-related costs were also described. The lecture-fee figure was RMB 2000, instructor accommodation was RMB 500, instructor meals were RMB 150, and instructor transportation was RMB 800. The instructor was described as coming from another location.

For approval materials, the original "Fudan University Approval Form for Engineering Project Payments and Large Cash Fund Payments" was provided; the form carried the official seal of "Fudan University Capital Construction Office"; and it also carried the "Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office". The project amount was marked as over RMB 100,000. The approval form had the Capital Construction Office director's signature. The approval form had the supervising vice president's signature.
Payment-control details: the requested payment followed the contract amount; the requested payment followed the contract progress; the file did not mark any overpayment; the file did not mark any advance payment across contract progress.
The case was marked as a first payment. The original "Fudan University Renovation Project Construction Contract" was provided. The first-payment request exceeded the contract ratio or amount.
The case was marked as a final settlement payment. The original "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" was provided. The original "Fudan University Construction Project Final Settlement Audit Filing Form" was provided. The cost-review opinion was stamped by the cost-review company. The cost-review opinion also carried the "Capital Construction Office Review Process" seal. The filing form was signed by the engineering management department. The filing form included the Audit Office signature and comments. The final payment was stated to follow the approved settlement total in the opinion. The final payment exceeded the contract price.

case_ch:
In February 2026, the Intelligent Sensing Lab prepared a campus repair-project settlement request. The payment materials were compiled by Dr. Wang under the engineering and renovation payment workflow.
For approval materials, the original "Fudan University Approval Form for Engineering Project Payments and Large Cash Fund Payments" was provided; the form carried the official seal of "Fudan University Capital Construction Office"; and it also carried the "Project Review Seal of the Fudan University Renovation Management Office". The project amount was marked as over RMB 100,000. The approval form had the Capital Construction Office director's signature. The approval form had the supervising vice president's signature.
Payment-control details: the requested payment followed the contract amount; the requested payment followed the contract progress; the file did not mark any overpayment; the file did not mark any advance payment across contract progress.
The case was marked as a first payment. The original "Fudan University Renovation Project Construction Contract" was provided. The first-payment request was not marked as exceeding the contract ratio or amount.
The case was marked as a final settlement payment. The original "Fudan University Construction Project Cost Review Opinion" was provided. The original "Fudan University Construction Project Final Settlement Audit Filing Form" was provided. The cost-review opinion was stamped by the cost-review company. The cost-review opinion also carried the "Capital Construction Office Review Process" seal. The filing form was signed by the engineering management department. The filing form included the Audit Office signature and comments. The final payment was stated to follow the approved settlement total in the opinion. The final payment was not marked as exceeding the contract price.

case_ch:
In February 2026, the Intelligent Sensing Lab submitted reimbursement materials for a domestic official hospitality event. The file was assembled by Dr. Zhou under the domestic official hospitality reimbursement workflow.
Event-level documentation: the case was processed on a one-event, one-settlement basis; financial invoices were attached; an official letter from the sending unit was attached; the official letter stated the purpose of the official activity; the official letter included the agenda or itinerary; the official letter included a visitor list; "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality List" was attached; "Fudan University Domestic Official Hospitality Approval Form" was attached.
Accommodation arrangements: no accommodation expense was separately claimed; accommodation was arranged for the visitors; the visitors did not include provincial- or ministerial-level guests; the rooms were arranged as standard rooms; no standard suite for provincial- or ministerial-level guests was shown; the visit was not from a Shanghai-based unit; no accommodation was arranged for a local Shanghai visit.
Meal and accompanying arrangements: a working meal was considered necessary; the meal format was boxed lunch or buffet; the daily meal standard was RMB 100 per person per day; the daily meal standard did not exceed RMB 100; one formal meal was arranged; the formal meal standard was RMB 150 per person; the formal meal standard did not exceed RMB 150; the visitor count was 9; the accompanying-person count was 3; the accompanying-person count was controlled; the simple-meal standard for accompanying staff was RMB 50 per person; that simple-meal standard did not exceed RMB 50.
Transportation arrangements: group transportation was arranged; the vehicle selection was reasonable; the number of accompanying vehicles was controlled.
natural_language_trace:
Evaluating Rule 18 (Official Hospitality Expense).

Step 1: Check — Basic requirements. Result: PASS.
Step 2: Check — Accommodation rules. Result: PASS.
Step 3: Check — Meal standards. Result: PASS.
Step 4: Check — Transportation rules. Result: PASS.
Step 5: Construct claim case. Output: Claim case constructed (category: Official Hospitality Fee).
Step 6: Check — Claim type ok rule18. Result: PASS.
Step 7: Conclusion — Full Approval.

case_ch:
In March 2026, the Intelligent Sensing Lab prepared a domestic visitor-reception reimbursement case. The packet was assembled by Dr. Zhou, and the file also carried related conference and travel-detail fields in the same combined record.
Visitor-reception details: the file stated that the reception was truthfully reported to the Foreign Affairs Office; "Fudan University Overseas Expert Work Expense Accounting Report" was attached; the responsible person signed the file; the handler signed the file; "Fudan University Domestic Reception List" was attached; the domestic reception list was signed by the responsible person; the domestic reception list included visitor organizations; the domestic reception list included visitor names; the domestic reception list included visit reasons.
Charge categories in the same record: lodging was claimed; intercity transport was claimed; daily meals were claimed; banquet expenses were claimed; local transport was claimed; gifts were claimed; staff mismeal subsidies were claimed.
Reception-standard details: the delegation was not led by a vice-ministerial-level official or above; the hotel standard was recorded as 4-star; the guest-level field was R19_Other; the daily meal standard was RMB 280 per person per day; the banquet type field was R19_Formal_Banquet; the banquet host was not ministerial or vice-ministerial level; the banquet standard was RMB 280 per person for one occasion; local transport complied with the level standard; the gift target-level field was R19_Gift_Bureau; the gift standard was RMB 180 per person for one occasion; the gift was not described as a small souvenir for other guests; the visit was not marked as a major diplomatic event for a head of state or government; the arrangement was not made under protocol requirements; the foreign-guest count was 4; the number of our accompanying participants was 4; the staff mismeal subsidy was RMB 50 per person per occasion.
The same file also contained conference-related fields: the visit was not conference-related; the conference file did not include lodging or meal expenses; the conference fund-type field was Research_Fund; the conference scope field was R9_Domestic_Business; the domestic conference class field was R9_Class1; the international conference class field was R9_Intl2; the attendee count was 100; the conference days field was 2 and the duration-days field was 2; lodging was arranged; meals were arranged; actual lodging was RMB 480 per person per day; actual meals were RMB 150 per person per day; actual other expenses were RMB 100 per person per day; actual total expenses were RMB 730 per person per day; excluded items stayed within budget; notice and agenda were attached; a budget form was attached; an expense detail list was attached; a sign-in sheet with organizations was attached; service invoices were attached; original supporting documents were attached; electronic settlement documents were attached; a contract or agreement was attached; the conference was reimbursed in a single batch; split reimbursement by channel was not needed; the file did not indicate that each channel was reimbursed once; previous channel details were not listed; the meeting was not marked as a regular work meeting or seminar; no separate work meal was claimed; no work-meal attendee list and detail sheet were attached; the work-meal standard field was RMB 0 per person; no advance payment was arranged; the advance ratio field was 0 percent; advance materials did not include the notice and agenda; advance materials did not include the budget form; advance materials did not include a related contract; advance materials did not include international approval where needed; foreign guests were not present at a domestic conference; the conference did not have Foreign Affairs Office approval; the Shanghai-based attendee ratio was 40 percent; the conference was not held outside Shanghai; the conference was not held in a prohibited scenic area.
The combined record also contained travel-expense fields: a trip report form was attached; the trip report form was signed by the unit head and sealed; this was not the unit head's own trip; no other-leader signature for a unit-head self-trip was shown; intercity transport expense was claimed; lodging expense was claimed; city transport expense was claimed; city transport receipts were provided; city transport stayed within budget; the traveler position field was R7P_Bureau_or_Equivalent_Duty; the chosen mode was Train; the chosen class was Train_First; the nightly lodging amount was RMB 600; the stay lasted 2 night(s); the city key was BJ_ALL; the travel month was 5; the trip covered 3 calendar day(s); the region type was Other_Region; the claimed meal subsidy total was RMB 300; the actual claimed city-transport total was RMB 180; the city-transport lump-sum claim was RMB 0.
